name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. provides cloud-based solutions for digital workflows across IT service management, customer service, HR, security operations, and enterprise-wide automation. The Now Platform enables organizations to digitize and automate workflows using AI-native capabilities. ServiceNow serves over 8,100 enterprise customers including approximately 85% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 2004, headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has more than doubled revenue from $7.2 billion in 2022 to $14 billion TTM, transforming from an IT service management tool into a comprehensive enterprise AI-native platform spanning customer service, HR, security, and field service [1]. The Now Platform expansion into agentic AI capabilities represents a genuine business model transformation — CEO McDermott stated the company has already eliminated 90% of customer service use cases that once required human workers [2]. AI Deployment (2/2): ServiceNow is shipping AI-powered products to enterprise customers at scale, with AI capabilities embedded across the platform including Now Assist, AI agents for IT workflows, and predictive intelligence [2]. The company expects same headcount in 2027 as 2026 through AI-driven productivity gains, demonstrating internal AI deployment with measurable results [2]. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow dominates the IT service management market and is the fastest-growing enterprise workflow platform, serving 85% of the Fortune 500 with 22% subscription revenue growth — no competitor matches this combination of scale and growth [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Raised full-year 2026 subscription revenue guidance to $15,735-15,775 million (22-22.5% growth), with Q2 2026 guided at $3,815-3,820 million; agentic AI capabilities expanding across all platform modules [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): ServiceNow invests heavily in R&D as a software company, with Brian Solis leading global innovation strategy and continuous platform enhancement across AI, automation, and workflow capabilities [2].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose is to 'make the world work better for everyone' — a specific, thoughtful mission that reflects genuine intentionality about transforming enterprise workflows to improve how organizations operate [2]. Under CEO Bill McDermott, this purpose has guided the expansion from IT into every enterprise function. The mission differentiates ServiceNow from generic software companies and connects technology to human productivity. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow has committed to 100% renewable electricity and carbon neutrality (achieved), with a net zero by 2030 target; executive compensation was tied to environmental and DEI targets [3]. However, no specific charitable foundation or large-scale community investment programs were identified. Employee Retention (2/2): ServiceNow has been recognized on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Fortune World's Most Admired Companies, and Glassdoor Best Places to Work lists [2]. Employees describe 'transparent leadership,' 'progressive culture,' and 'super smart and talented grown ups with no massive politics,' indicating above-average retention for the enterprise software sector. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO McDermott's compensation is heavily equity-based and he has maintained significant ServiceNow holdings, signaling alignment with long-term shareholder value, though no notable net insider purchases beyond compensation were identified [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3,671 million grew 22% YoY, beating the high end of guidance across all metrics, and the company raised full-year guidance [1]. D/E of 20.7% is excellent (score 1.0). ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target (score 1.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% far exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0). Revenue growth 3-year CAGR of 22.4% (score 1.0, 2x weight). Forward P/E of 21.1 is slightly above the 20 target (score 0.97). Profit margin of 12.6% is below the 20% target (score 0.63). PEG of 1.11 is near the 1.0 target (score 0.95, 2x weight). Free cash flow of $5.1 billion demonstrates exceptional cash generation [1].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): ServiceNow occupies a unique position in the AI disruption landscape — it is simultaneously a beneficiary of AI (embedding AI into its platform to increase value) and partially exposed to AI substitution risk, as AI-native workflow tools from Microsoft Copilot, Salesforce Agentforce, and startup competitors could potentially replicate ServiceNow's automation capabilities without the platform lock-in [2]. The company's deep enterprise integrations, compliance workflows, and 8,100+ customer relationships create switching costs that protect the installed base. However, AI's commoditization of basic IT service management and workflow automation could compress pricing power over time. ServiceNow's proactive AI strategy — eliminating 90% of customer service use cases and planning flat headcount — demonstrates awareness and adaptation [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow is generating meaningful AI revenue through Now Assist, AI agents embedded across IT, HR, customer service, and security modules, with AI capabilities driving premium pricing and upsell opportunities across the installed base [1][2].

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott plays a primarily infinite game, with a genuine purpose ('make the world work better for everyone') that guides platform expansion decisions and a culture recognized on multiple best-workplace lists. McDermott's vision is bold and consistently articulated, and the company's willingness to cannibalize its own customer service use cases through AI demonstrates courage. However, McDermott's candid AI job displacement commentary (30% Gen-Z unemployment prediction) and the operational intensity of quarterly beat-and-raise guidance create tension between infinite purpose and finite execution.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'Make the world work better for everyone' is a genuine, distinctive purpose that has guided ServiceNow's expansion from IT ticketing into enterprise-wide workflow automation across every organizational function [2]. Under McDermott (CEO since 2019), the cause has survived the COVID transition, competitive threats from Microsoft and Salesforce, and the AI revolution. Strategic decisions — expanding into HR, customer service, security, field service — trace back to this purpose. However, ServiceNow is only 22 years old and hasn't yet demonstrated the multi-decade, multi-CEO consistency Sinek requires for the highest score.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow is recognized on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For and Glassdoor Best Places to Work lists, with employees consistently citing transparent leadership, progressive culture, and minimal internal politics [2]. McDermott's leadership style is described as energetic and vision-driven, creating a sense of shared purpose across the 29,187-person organization. Brian Solis as head of global innovation demonstrates investment in empowering creative thinking at the leadership level.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott explicitly acknowledged that 'Silicon Valley is getting enterprise AI wrong' while positioning ServiceNow's differentiated approach, demonstrating competitive awareness without seeking to destroy rivals [2]. ServiceNow's competitive response to Microsoft Copilot and Salesforce Agentforce has been to improve its own AI capabilities rather than engage in market-share warfare, focusing on what makes the Now Platform uniquely valuable.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow has evolved significantly from an IT service management tool to an AI-native enterprise platform, with the deliberate decision to embed AI agents that eliminate traditional human-performed workflows representing a meaningful strategic evolution [1][2]. However, this is an expansion of the core platform rather than a true existential pivot — ServiceNow has not abandoned a profitable business line in service of its cause.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): McDermott has been remarkably candid about AI's workforce impact, publicly predicting 30% Gen-Z unemployment and stating ServiceNow will maintain flat headcount through AI-driven attrition [2]. The decision to eliminate 90% of customer service use cases through AI, effectively cannibalizing traditional service revenue, demonstrates willingness to embrace disruptive change. However, these decisions also serve quarterly financial performance rather than requiring genuine sacrifice.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered an exceptional Q1 2026, beating the high end of guidance across all metrics with subscription revenue of $3,671 million growing 22% YoY and raising full-year subscription guidance to $15,735-15,775 million [1]. The company generates $5.1 billion in free cash flow, maintains a nearly debt-free balance sheet (D/E 20.7%, $5.2 billion cash vs. $2.4 billion debt), and serves 85% of the Fortune 500 with deeply embedded workflows. At a PEG of 1.11, ServiceNow offers rare growth-at-a-reasonable-price in enterprise software — 22% revenue growth at just 21x forward earnings. The AI-native platform strategy is driving premium pricing and upsell opportunities, with agentic AI capabilities positioned to capture the enterprise automation wave [2].
thesis_continued: The primary concern is margin progression and valuation sustainability. Profit margin of 12.6% is below enterprise software peers, and trailing P/E of 63x reflects the market pricing in significant margin expansion that must materialize [1]. GAAP EPS grew only 2.3% YoY despite 22% revenue growth, indicating that investment spending is consuming top-line gains. Competition from Microsoft Copilot (bundled with M365), Salesforce Agentforce, and AI-native startups could compress ServiceNow's pricing power in workflow automation. McDermott's public prediction of 30% Gen-Z unemployment from AI may generate reputational headwinds and regulatory scrutiny [2]. The stock trades 50% below its 52-week high of $211.48, suggesting the market has already repriced growth expectations, but further multiple compression is possible if AI-driven productivity gains fail to translate into operating leverage.
key_catalysts: Raised full-year 2026 subscription revenue guidance to $15,735-15,775 million (22-22.5% growth), with Q2 guided at $3,815-3,820 million — continued beat-and-raise cadence could re-rate the stock
Agentic AI capabilities expanding across all platform modules, driving premium pricing and net-new customer wins as enterprises prioritize workflow automation for cost reduction
Operating leverage inflection as AI-driven productivity enables flat headcount while revenue grows 22%, potentially accelerating margin expansion toward enterprise software benchmarks
key_risks: Trailing P/E of 63x and profit margin of 12.6% require significant margin expansion to justify the valuation — any deceleration in revenue growth or operating leverage could trigger multiple compression
Microsoft Copilot bundled with M365, Salesforce Agentforce, and AI-native workflow startups pose competitive threats that could commoditize basic IT service management capabilities

McDermott's public AI job displacement predictions (30% Gen-Z unemployment) may attract regulatory scrutiny and reputational risk, particularly as enterprise customers face public pressure on responsible AI adoption
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Earnings Release and Full Year 2026 Guidance — SEC Form 8-K, April 2026
ServiceNow Leadership, AI Strategy, and Corporate Culture — Fortune, CNBC, Fast Company, Glassdoor, 2025-2026
ServiceNow ESG Commitments and Sustainability Goals — ServiceNow corporate site, SEC DEF 14A, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed June 2026
